hero.subheadline: ELPRESY leverages CART Decision Tree Regression to forecast your air conditioner's daily ampere consumption from just two inputs — enabling data-driven energy optimization for academic research and real-world application.

features.heading: Everything You Need to Analyze AC Energy Consumption
features.subheading: A comprehensive suite of tools built for rigorous academic analysis and practical energy monitoring.

predictionEngine.title:
CART Decision Tree
Regression Engine
predictionEngine.description: Powered by the ml-cart algorithm, our prediction engine delivers high-accuracy estimations using robust machine learning models optimized for continuous variables.

charts.title: Charts & Visualization
charts.description: Interactive charts that map out your predictions over time, making it easy to spot trends and anomalies.

history.title: History & Log
history.description: A detailed repository of all past predictions. Filter, search, and review historical data with ease.

export.title: Export Data
export.description: Export your findings instantly to CSV and PDF formats for academic reporting and further analysis.

multiLanguage.title: Multi-language
multiLanguage.description: Fully localized interface supporting both Indonesian and English to suit your audience.

howItWorks.heading: From Input to Insight in Three Steps
howItWorks.subheading: Our streamlined process makes complex machine learning predictions accessible and actionable.

step1.title: Input Variables
step1.description: Enter your AC's ampere per cycle and the total daily usage hours into the system.

step2.title: Run Prediction
step2.description: The CART Decision Tree Regression model processes your inputs to calculate the expected usage.

step3.title: Analyze Results
step3.description: View detailed charts, track historical performance, and export your data for further analysis.

howItWorks.poweredBy: Powered by ml-cart — CART Decision Tree Regression algorithm

preview.heading: A Research Tool Built for Precision
preview.subheading: Experience an interface designed for academic rigor without compromising on modern usability.

research.heading: The Research Behind ELPRESY
research.subheading: Understanding why predicting AC ampere usage matters — and how ELPRESY approaches it.
research.quote: AC units account for 40–60% of residential electricity consumption in tropical climates.
research.paragraph1: In Indonesia and other tropical nations, air conditioning is not a luxury — it's a necessity. Yet most households have little visibility into how much energy their AC units actually draw on a daily basis. ELPRESY was built to close that gap.
research.paragraph2: By applying machine learning to just two easily measurable inputs, the system delivers reliable daily ampere forecasts without requiring specialized metering equipment or electrical expertise.
research.objective1: Predict daily AC ampere usage with high reliability based on minimal input variables.
research.objective2: Visualize consumption trends over time to identify inefficiencies and usage patterns.
research.objective3: Provide exportable data logging in CSV and PDF formats for continuous academic analysis.
research.methodology: CART (Classification and Regression Trees) via the ml-cart library, configured for regression on continuous variables with pruning to prevent overfitting.

author.heading: The Mind Behind ELPRESY
author.name: [Your Name]
author.bio: This application serves as the practical implementation of my undergraduate thesis. It aims to demonstrate the viability of CART Decision Tree Regression in forecasting electrical consumption, providing an intuitive interface for a complex machine learning model.
